complaint_details.how_fraud_discovered: When I requested withdrawal of Rs 3,00,000 from the app, the request was shown as pending. When I contacted Rohit Verma, he demanded Rs 50,000 as tax clearance fee claiming it was mandatory under SEBI rules. I checked online and confirmed SEBI does not charge any such fee. I then tried calling the SEBI helpline number Rohit had given me (1800-XXX-XXXX) and it was not a real SEBI number. I called the real National Cyber Crime Helpline 1930 and filed this complaint.
evidence_provided_by_victim: ProfitSure Pro app screenshots showing fake portfolio
Fake SEBI certificate PDF received from accused
Bank statement from SBI showing debits

narrative_statement: I, Priya Sharma, resident of Sector 14 Gurugram, state that on 1st November 2024, I received an unsolicited WhatsApp message from phone number +918765432109. The person identified himself as Rohit Verma and claimed to be a SEBI registered investment advisor. He sent me a PDF document showing his SEBI registration certificate and a brochure for an investment platform called ProfitSure Pro. Over the next few days, he called me multiple times and convinced me that his platform provides guaranteed returns of 30-40% per month through algo-trading technology. He asked me to download the ProfitSure Pro app from a link he sent on WhatsApp and transfer money to an account named RS Trading Services at ICICI Bank. Between 7th November and 12th November 2024, I transferred a total of Rs 4,70,000 across three UPI transactions. The app showed that my portfolio was growing rapidly. On 14th November, when I tried to withdraw Rs 3,00,000, the app showed the withdrawal as pending. When I contacted Rohit, he told me I need to pay Rs 50,000 as tax clearance fee, claiming it is a government requirement for high-value withdrawals. I realized this was a fraud. I called the national cyber crime helpline 1930 and reported the incident. I request the police to investigate this matter, recover my money, and take action against the accused.
